response.justification: The candidate brings 15+ years of broad software engineering experience with genuine open-source contributions and Linux familiarity, which align with parts of the role. However, there is a hard disqualifier: German proficiency is listed as 'Basic,' whereas the position mandates a minimum C1 level for client-facing consulting work. Beyond the language gap, the resume reflects a career of strong individual-contributor roles rather than explicit team leadership or Head of Development responsibility, and there is no evidence of UML expertise, formal requirements management, or structured project management — all listed as required qualifications.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse, hands-on engineering experience spanning web, embedded, mobile, and distributed systems across multiple languages and stacks
Active open-source maintainer (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial) with demonstrated commitment to Linux/Unix environments and community knowledge-sharing
Staff-level roles and community workshop facilitation (TDD, Web Internals, Selenium courses) indicate capacity to guide and upskill others
concerns: German proficiency is self-reported as 'Basic,' which does not meet the mandatory C1 minimum — a non-negotiable requirement for this client-facing consulting role
No formal team leadership, people management, or Head of Development title appears anywhere in the employment history; roles are consistently individual-contributor or founding-solo-dev
No mention of UML, requirements management frameworks, IT security/risk/compliance methodologies, or structured architectural design processes that the role explicitly requires

key_factors.factor: Absence of formal leadership and architecture track record
